Name: 5110, 5167 Foot
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTES:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If loss of use is caused by a nerve condition the Veteran will receive a single rating under this schedule or under the appropriate Nerve schedule; whichever gives the higher rating.; In the event the Veteran has a bilateral foot condition and one foot is amputated/considered loss of use, then the VA will award a single 40% for the loss of use foot and a separate rating the non-amputated/loss of use foot.; For example, if the Veteran has bilateral flat foot and the left foot is considered loss of use. Then the VA will rate the remaining right flat foot without consideration of the left foot. In short, consider the left foot as being “normal” when evaluating the right flat foot.; NOTES:; *Veteran is entitled to Special Monthly Compensation.

Name: Veterans who also have an amputation or loss of use of a hand will have both ratings replaced with a singular 100% rating.
Forefoot Amputation
Description: NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Veteran is entitled to Special Monthly Compensation.; Toe Amputations; Amputation of toes is rated on the number of toes lost (on the SAME foot), the degree of amputation, and if the big toe is involved.; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; Only one of the following amputation ratings may be given per foot.; Return to top

Name: Policeman's Heel (Plantar Fasciitis)
Description: When the Veteran has severe pain in the heel of their foot.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTES:; *Per foot.; **Veteran is entitled to Special Monthly Compensation.; If the Veteran is NOT a candidate for surgery but it was recommended, for rating purposes they are to be considered to have tried it.

Name: Flat Foot (Pes Planus)
Description: When the Veteran's foot has no or very low arches.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Veteran is rated based upon the severity of their disability:; Mild; symptoms relieved by built-up shoe or arch support.; Moderate; weight-bearing line over or medial to great toe, inward bowing of the tendo achillis, pain on manipulation and use of the feet.; Severe; objective evidence of marked deformity (pronation, abduction, etc.), pain on manipulation and use accentuated, indication of swelling on use, characteristic callosities.; Pronounced; marked pronation, extreme tenderness of plantar surfaces of the feet, marked inward displacement and severe spasm of the tendo achillis on manipulation, not improved by orthopedic shoes or appliances.

Name: Claw Foot (Pes Cavus)
Description: When the Veteran's foot has arches that are too high.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Veteran is rated based upon the severity of their disability:; Slight; Symptoms relieved by built-up shoe or arch support.; Moderate; Great toe dorsiflexed, some limitation of dorsiflexion at ankle, definite tenderness under metatarsal heads.; Severe; All toes tending to dorsiflexion, limitation of dorsiflexion at ankle to right angle, shortened plantar fascia, and marked tenderness under metatarsal heads.; Pronounced; Marked contraction of plantar fascia with dropped forefoot, all toes hammer toes, very painful callosities, marked varus deformity.; PYRAMIDING NOTES:; Veterans CANNOT receive a claw foot rating and a Hammer toe rating for the same foot.; Veterans CANNOT receive a claw foot rating and a Hallux rigidus rating for the same foot.

Name: Morton's Disease (Metatarsalgia, Anterior)
Description: When a small painful mass forms over the nerves between the toes, under the foot.

Name: Bunions (Hallux Valgus)
Description: A bony bump on the outside of the big toe.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTE:; Veterans CAN receive a separate Flat foot rating and a hallux valgus rating for the same foot.

Name: Stiff Big Toe (Hallux Rigidus)
Description: When bone spurs cause an arthritis form at the base of the big toe. Resulting in the toe becoming stiff and difficult to move.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTES:; Veterans CANNOT receive a separate claw foot rating and a hallux rigidus rating for the same foot.; Veterans CANNOT receive a separate hallux rigidus rating and a Hammer toe rating for the same foot.

Name: Hammer Toe
Description: When the toe is permanently bent. Giving the appearance that the toe is shaped like the head of hammer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTES:; Veterans CANNOT receive a separate claw foot rating and a hammer toe rating for the same foot.; Veterans CANNOT receive a separate hallux rigidus rating and a hammer toe rating for the same foot.

Name: Foot Bones (Tarsal or Metatarsal Bones)
Description: If a bone in the foot is broken and healed improperly or never heals it will be rated upon how it affects the function of the foot.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: *Veteran is entitled to Special Monthly Compensation.; Return to top

Name: Bilateral Weak Foot
Description: A symptomatic condition secondary to many constitutional conditions, characterized by atrophy of the musculature, disturbed circulation, and weakness.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: When both feet are affected then whatever is the primary condition CANNOT be rated less than 10%.; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; This is NOT an additional individual rating!; RATER NOTE:; On a codesheet this would be coded as 52XX-5277 (with the Xs being the code for the foot condition which is causing the bilateral weak feet).

Name: Catch All (Foot Injuries, Other)
Description: When a foot condition exists but does not fit under any other criteria. It will be rated on how the severity affects the foot.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; *Veteran is entitled to Special Monthly Compensation.; Return to top; PYRAMIDING NOTES:; The Veteran will NOT receive an additional rating if they fit the following criteria. The Veteran will get a single rating under the rating schedule for their foot condition or this rating; Whichever rating is higher!; If feet conditions do NOT violate the General or other pyramiding rules then a 10% can be awarded for each condition based on pain with motion.; If the foot condition which applies the miscellaneous rating mentions bilateral in the schedule then only a single 10% can be awarded based on pain with motion!; So no 10% per foot!; Arthritis; When a Veteran's arthritis does not warrant a rating based upon ROM or other miscellaneous situations. The Veteran's condition will be rated under degenerative arthritis.; For an explanation of arthritis ratings click HERE.; Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veteran's may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. Name: Sickle Cell Anemia (Sickle Cell Disease)
Description: Anemia where the red blood cells become shaped like a sickle.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; In order to get service-connection the Veteran MUST have served in a location that can be linked as to the cause of the sickle cell. This is due to the fact that sickle cell can be caused by the Veteran's own genes.

Name: Aplastic Anemia (Bone Marrow Aplasia)
Description: Bone marrow not making enough new blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; The 100% evaluation for peripheral blood or bone marrow stem cell transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.

Name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
Description: Occurs when your body doesn't have enough iron.

Name: Addison's Anemia (Pernicious Anemia, Vitamin B12 Deficiency Anemia)
Description: When the body can't absorb enough vitamin B-12.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; This 100% rating IS temporary, and will continue for 6 months after release from hospital or at the end of the parenteral B12 therapy. At which time the Veteran will be Reevaluated. This condition will then receive a 10% rating, if the 100% criteria is no longer meet.; The following secondary conditions CAN receive their own individual ratings:; Dementia; Peripheral neuropathy; Myelopathy; Related gastrointestinal issues

Name: Acquired Hemolytic Anemia (Extrinsic Hemolytic Anemia)
Description: IMPORTANT NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: The 100% evaluation for bone marrow transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; Return to top; Carries gasses throughout the blood.

Name: Polycythemia Vera
Description: Bone marrow produces too many red blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; The 100% evaluation IS TEMPORARY and will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital/treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; Chemotherapy (including myelosuppressants) warrants a 100% evaluation for 6 months after treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; If this condition becomes cancerous the Veteran will be rated under Leukemia instead of this schedule.; For the following conditions. The Veteran can receive individual ratings for each condition!; Gout; Hypertension; Stroke; Thrombotic disease

Name: Folate Deficiency (Folic Acid Deficiency)
Description: When the body doesn't have enough folic acid, resulting in low number of red blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; Stuff that helps your immune system.

Name: Agranulocytosis (Agranulosis, Granulopenia, Leukopenia)
Description: Condition where the bone marrow does not produce enough white blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; The 100% evaluation for bone marrow transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.

Name: Chronic Myelogenous Leukemia (CML, Chronic Myeloid Leukemia, Chronic Granulocytic Leukemia)
Description: Condition where the bone marrow produces too many white blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; The 100% evaluation for peripheral blood or bone marrow stem cell transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; Chemotherapy (including myelosuppressants) or interferon treatment warrants a 100% evaluation for 6 months after treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; If this condition becomes cancerous the Veteran will be rated under Leukemia instead of this schedule.; Return to top; Helps stop bleeding by forming blood clots.

Name: Low Platelet Level (Immune Thrombocytopenia, Thrombocytopenia)
Description: IMPORTANT NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: The 100% evaluation for chemotherapy will continue for 6 months after treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.

Name: Essential Thrombocythemia & Primary Myelofibrosis
Description: Body produces too many platelets. | Bone marrow is unable to make enough normal blood cells
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; The 100% evaluation for peripheral blood or bone marrow stem cell transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; Chemotherapy (including myelosuppressants) or interferon treatment warrants a 100% evaluation for 6 months after treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; If this condition becomes cancerous the Veteran will be rated under Leukemia instead of this schedule.; Return to top

Name: Removal of Spleen - Full or Partial (Splenectomy)
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Veteran's experiencing any lasting residuals can have those rated separately.; Filters the blood, removing old red blood cells.

Name: Other Spleen Conditions (Injury of the Spleen)
Description: If the spleen causes symptoms that affect the Veteran. Those symptoms may be rated as appropriate.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top

Name: Tuberculous Adenitis (Tuberculosis of the Lymph Nodes, TB Adenitis, Crofula)
Description: Tuberculous infection of the lymph nodes in the neck.

Name: Primary Amyloidosis (AL Amyloidosis, Primary Systemic Amyloidosis, PSA, Amyloid Light-Chain Amyloidosis)
Description: When the body produces abnormal proteins that can build up and interfere with the functioning of various organs.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.; Return to top

Name: Leukemia (Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia, CLL)
Description: Cancer of the blood or bone marrow that causes the over-production of immature white blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; While the condition may be ‘active’ if the condition is considered “asymptomatic, Rai Stage 0” then a 0%, NOT 100% will be awarded!; Chronic myelogenous leukemia is NOT rated under this schedule.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.; If exposed to Burn pits.; Residuals will be rated under one or more of the following schedules:; Chronic myelogenous leukemia;; Essential thrombocythemia and primary myelofibrosis; or; Polycythemia vera.

Name: Hodgkin’s Disease (Hodgkin's Lymphoma)
Description: Cancer of the part of the immune system called the lymphatic system.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.; If exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma
Description: Lymphomas that are not Hodgkin’s.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.; If exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Myelodysplastic Syndrome (MDS, Preleukemia)
Description: Blood cancer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; The 100% evaluation for peripheral blood or bone marrow stem cell transplant will continue for 6 months after the Veteran is released from the hospital. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; Chemotherapy warrants a 100% evaluation for 6 months after treatment ends. After which, the Veteran will have a MANDATORY Reevaluation.; If this condition becomes cancerous the Veteran will be rated under Leukemia instead of this schedule.

Name: Kahler's Disease (Bone Marrow Cancer, Multiple Myeloma)
Description: A cancer of plasma cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; 5 YEARS from the time of diagnosis WITH symptoms is when a MANDATORY Reevaluation is required.; Meaning if you are service-connected 4 years AFTER symptoms start, you will be reevaluated in a year.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.

Name: Solitary Plasmacytoma
Description: Tumor composed of plasma cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veteran's may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. Name: Cholera (Asiatic Cholera) & Vibriosis (Non-Cholera)
Description: Infection of the small intestine by some strains of the bacterium Vibrio cholerae.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Kidney damage; Musculoskeletal conditions; Skin damage

Name: Black Fever (Dumdum Fever, Kala Azar, Visceral Leishmaniasis)
Description: Parasitic disease.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Bone marrow disease; Liver damage

Name: Leprosy (Hansen’s Disease)
Description: Infection caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium leprae. It mainly affects the skin, eyes, nose and peripheral nerves.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Has an established incubation period of 5 or more years.; Skin damage; Nerve damage

Name: Malaria
Description: Parasitic disease that infects red blood cells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; This is a Presumptive condition for those who served in the Tropical regions.; Liver damage; Nerve damage; Spleen damage

Name: Lymphatic Filariasis (Elephantiasis, Filariasis)
Description: Parasitic disease that infects the lymphatic system, including the lymph nodes.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Epididymitis; Lymphangitis; Lymphatic obstruction; Lymphedema affecting extremities, genitals, and/or breasts

Name: Bartonellosis
Description: Disease caused by the bacteria of the genus Bartonella.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Can cause many other diseases such as:; Bacillary angiomatosis; Carrion’s disease; Cat scratch fever (Lymphoreticulosis); Chronic bacteremia; chronic lymphadenopathy; Trench fever; Endocarditis; Neurological conditions; Skin damage

Name: Plague
Description: A rare but serious bacterial infection.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Amputations; Nerve conditions

Name: Relapsing Fever
Description: Recurring fever disease caused by several species of the bacteria Spirochete borrelia that are transmitted by lice or ticks.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Eye conditions - iritis, uveitis; Liver damage; Nerve damage; Spleen damage

Name: Rheumatic Fever
Description: A disease that can result from inadequately treated strep throat or scarlet fever
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Heart damage

Name: Syphilis & Other Treponema Infections
Description: A bacterial infection usually spread by sexual contact.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Commonly rated under the following schedules:; Cerebrospinal Syphilis;; Dementia due to central nervous system syphilis;; Meningovascular Syphilis;; Syphilitic heart disease; or; Tabes Dorsalis.; Otherwise the condition is rated based upon residuals.; Damage of the eyes; Internal ear damage; Heart damage; Nerve damage

Name: Nontuberculosis Mycobacterium Infection
Description: A bacterium within the same family that causes tuberculosis. But this bacterium itself does not cause TB.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Central nervous system conditions; Digestive conditions; Eye conditions; Genitourinary conditions; Musculoskeletal issues; Respiratory issues; Skin conditions

Name: Typhus & Anaplasma Infections
Description: Diseases caused by the bacteria Anaplasma phagocytophilum, Rickettsia, or Orientia
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This includes diseases such as:; African tick-borne fever; Anaplasmosis; Bush typhus; Ehrlichia; Ehrlichiosis; Rickettsial; Rocky Mountain spotted fever; Scrub Typhus; Bone marrow conditions; Central nervous system conditions; Skin damage; Spleen damage

Name: Whitmore's Disease (Melioidosis)
Description: Disease caused by the bacterium Burkholderia pseudomallei.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Arthritis; Lung lesions; Meningitis

Name: Lyme Disease (Borreliosis)
Description: Disease caused by the bacterium Borrelia.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Arthritis; Bell's palsy; Chronic fatigue; Cognitive dysfunction; Heart damage; Radiculopathy; Vision problems

Name: Hyperinfection Syndrome & Disseminated Strongyloidiasis
Description: When your immune system is unable to fight back parasitic invasions effectively.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; The immune system itself is NOT compromised by the parasites themselves.

Name: Snail Fever (Bilharzia, Schistosomiasis)
Description: Disease caused by parasitic flatworms called schistosome.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Central nervous system damage; Female genital tract issues; Genitourinary issues; Intestinal system issues; Liver conditions

Name: Hemorrhagic Fevers, including Dengue, Yellow Fever, and others
Description: Any of a group of infectious diseases that interfere with the blood's ability to clot.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Central nervous system damage; Kidney conditions; Liver conditions

Name: Campylobacter Jejuni Infection
Description: Disease caused by bacteria of the genus Campylobacter.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Arthritis; Eye conditions; Guillan-Barre Syndrome

Name: Q Fever (Coxiella Burnetii Infection, Query Fever)
Description: An infection caused by the bacterium Coxiella burnetii.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Artery/Vein conditions; Chronic fatigue; Chronic hepatitis; Endocarditis; Osteomyelitis

Name: Nontyphoid Salmonella Infections
Description: Nontyphoidal salmonellae are gram-negative bacteria.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Arthritis

Name: Shigella Infections (Shigellosis)
Description: Caused by Shigella bacteria.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Arthritis; Kidney damage

Name: West Nile Virus Infection
Description: West Nile virus (WNV) is the leading cause of mosquito-borne disease in the continental United States.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Mental disorders; Musculoskeletal issues

Name: All Other Parasitic Diseases
Description: Catchall for any unnamed parasitic diseases.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Liver damage; Return to top; Disease caused by the bacteria Mycobacterium tuberculosis.; There are essentially two forms of TB:; Affect lungs (pulmonary); Affect other organs (non-pulmonary); NOTE:; There are two schedules to rate pulmonary TB. The difference being those rated before or after August 19, 1968. However, the old schedule will NOT be referenced as any Veterans who were rated under that old schedule will have had their ratings considered Protected long ago.

Name: Miliary Tuberculosis
Description: Form of TB that starts in the lungs and then spreads to other organs.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; If the Veteran has active/inactive TB in the lungs and/or other organs. The Veteran can be rated immediately based upon their secondary symptoms without having to wait for the temporary 100% rating to end.; NOTE:; This is NOT a misspelling of ‘military’.; Central nervous system conditions; Digestive conditions; Eye conditions; Genitourinary conditions; Musculoskeletal issues; Respiratory issues; Skin conditions

Name: Tuberculous Pleurisy (Pulmonary Tuberculosis)
Description: Swelling of the lungs due to liquid build up between the layers of the lining of the lungs with hard growths on the lining of the lungs.

Name: All Other Active Pulmonary Tuberculoses
Description: Note:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Active pulmonary tuberculosis will be considered Permanently and totally disabling for Non-service-connected pension purposes in one of the following circumstances:; Associated with active tuberculosis involving other than the respiratory system;; With severe associated symptoms or with extensive cavity formation;; Reactivated cases;; With advancement of lesions on successive examinations or while under treatment; or; Without retrogression of lesions or other evidence of material improvement at the end of 6 months hospitalization or without change of diagnosis from “active” at the end of 12 months hospitalization. Material improvement means lessening or absence of clinical symptoms, and X-ray findings of a stationary or retrogressive lesion.

Name: Inactive Pulmonary Tuberculosis
Description: NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If a Rib was removed (thoracoplasty) the rib may be rated separately.; Tuberculosis Affecting Other Organs (Nonpulmonary); Return to top; Infectious conditions that can affect the immune system.

Name: Lupus (Systematic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE))
Description: An inflammatory disease caused when the immune system attacks its own healthy tissues.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; This schedule is for lupus that attacks tissues within the body NOT the Skin!; PYRAMIDING NOTES:; You CANNOT get separate ratings for lupus that affects the Skin and for lupus that affects tissues within the body. If the Veteran suffers from both, they will get the higher of the two ratings.; If the Veteran can get a higher rating based upon residual symptoms of conditions that develop secondary to the lupus. The Veteran CANNOT get a rating for lupus.

Name: HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) & AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome)
Description: Virus that attacks the body's immune system. If HIV is not treated, it can lead to AIDS.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: RATER NOTE:; If there is evidence indicating that the HIV/AIDS was the result of intravenous drug abuse. The VA must then make a willful misconduct/line-of-duty (LOD) administrative decision prior to rating, to determine if the Veteran is eligible for service-connection.; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; Mental illnesses, Central nervous system manifestations, opportunistic infections, and tumors MAY be rated separately if a higher overall rating results, provided the disability symptoms do NOT overlap with the rating criteria below.; *Minimum rating for someone with a T4 cell count of less than 200.; **Minimum rating for someone with AIDS-related opportunistic disease/infection or tumor(s).; IMPORTANT NOTE:; If the Veteran has a T4 count below the threshold for a medical diagnosis of AIDS BUT has a diagnosis of one or more of the following diseases they are considered to have a diagnosis of AIDS, for VA purposes!

Name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS, Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME), Systemic Exertion Intolerance Disease (SEID))
Description: A disorder characterized by extreme fatigue or tiredness that doesn't go away with rest and can't be explained by an underlying medical condition.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; For this schedule, incapacitation exists only when a licensed physician prescribes bed rest and treatment.; This is a syndrome that can only be diagnosed via a process of elimination, as there are many different conditions that have symptoms of significant fatigue. For a proper diagnosis FOR VA PURPOSES the condition persist for at least 6 months; AND; There MUST be at least 6 of the following symptoms present:; Body aches;; Cognitive problems (concentration issues, forgetfulness, confusion);; Debilitating fatigue lasting 24 hours or more after exercise;; Fever;; Headaches;; Joint pain;; Muscle aches;; Sore throat;; Tender lymph nodes; or; Weakness.; Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veterans may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. For more information on DBQs click HERE.; Chronic Fatigue Syndrome; HIV-Related Illnesses; Infectious Diseases (other than HIV-related illness chronic fatigue syndrome and tuberculosis); Persian Gulf/Afghanistan Infectious Diseases; Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) and other Autoimmune Diseases; Tuberculosis; Return to top; Click HERE to view the Master Condition List.; Return to top; 38 CFR § 4.88a Chronic fatigue syndrome; 38 CFR § 4.88b Schedule of ratings—infectious diseases, immune disorders and nutritional deficiencies; 38 CFR § 4.88c Ratings for inactive nonpulmonary tuberculosis initially entitled after August 19, 1968; 38 CFR § 4.96 Special provisions regarding evaluation of respiratory conditions; 38 CFR § 4.97 Schedule of ratings—respiratory system; Return to top; Homepage Link; Sub-Reddit:r/VeteransBenefits; Discord server: U.S. Veterans Network; © 2021-2025 Veterans Benefits Knowledge Base, all rights reserved.

Name: Diabetes Mellitus (Type 1 Diabetes, Type 2 Diabetes)
Description: When the pancreas does not produce enough insulin (Type 1); or
Medical Terms and Symptoms: When the body does not respond properly to insulin (Type 2).; IMPORTANT NOTES:; It is the Department of Veteran’s Affairs (VA’s) policy to concede that an individual’s diabetes mellitus requires insulin, restricted diet, and regulation of activities when the diabetes has caused episodes of ketoacidosis that have resulted in hospitalization.; Gestational diabetes (Type 3) diabetes that can occur during pregnancy CANNOT be given a rating as the condition is temporary.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If diagnosed within a Year of separation AND the condition rates at least 10%; or; If exposed to Agent Orange.; Definition:; Regulation of activities -; Voluntary avoidance of strenuous activity with the intention of avoiding hypoglycemic episodes, does NOT meet the regulatory criteria.; Prescribed or voluntary exercise also does NOT satisfy the regulation-of-activities criterion.; Evidence MUST document that the avoidance of strenuous activities is required/prescribed as part of medical management of the individual’s diabetes.; IMPORTANT NOTE:; If the secondary condition(s) rates 0% then it will NOT get a separate rating! It will be displayed as: Diabetes Type II with X, Y, etc. That said, you will STILL be service-connected for the condition(s)!; ; Erectile dysfunction; Eye issues - retinopathy, blindness, cataracts, glaucoma; Heart conditions - coronary artery disease, heart attack, atherosclerosis; High blood pressure; Kidney issues - nephropathy; Mental disorders - dementia, depression; Nerve issues - neuropathy; Skin ulcer; Stroke; Return to top; Helps regulate many of the body’s processes, such as metabolism, energy generation, and mood.

Name: Overactive Thyroid (Hyperthyroidism, including Graves' Disease)
Description: Thyroid produces too many hormones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Double Vision; Hyperthyroid heart disease; Vision problems

Name: Toxic Thyroid Enlargement
Description: When the condition causes the thyroid to enlarge (tumors, nodules, swelling, etc.) AND makes the thyroid produce too many hormones (hyperthyroidism).
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Difficulty swallowing; Disfigurement of the neck; Double Vision; Hyperthyroid heart disease; Vision problems

Name: Nontoxic Thyroid Enlargement
Description: When the condition causes the thyroid to enlarge (tumors, nodules, swelling, etc.) AND does NOT make the thyroid produce too many hormones (hyperthyroidism).
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If the swelling causes Disfigurement of the neck or if it puts Too much pressure on the esophagus, making it hard to swallow. The Veteran may get individual ratings for those conditions.

Name: Underactive Thyroid (Hypothyroidism)
Description: Thyroid produces too few hormones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Agent Orange.; Myxedema - Symptoms include:; Cold intolerance; Constipation; Dementia; Depression; Feeling cold; Heart conditions (low blood pressure, slow heart rate, and fluid buildup around the heart); Muscle cramps; Muscle weakness; Stupor (slowed thought); Tiredness; Weight gain; Eye conditions - such as blurred or double vision; Digestive conditions; Mental disorders

Name: Thyroiditis
Description: Thyroid gland becomes irritated and swells.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; If it causes Hyperthyroidism or Hypothyroidism it will be rated under those schedules.; Return to top; Controls the amount of calcium and phosphate in the body.

Name: Overactive Parathyroid (Hyperparathyroidism)
Description: Parathyroid produces too many hormones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Hypercalcemia - too much calcium in the blood. For the purposes of the VA the Veteran must meet 1 or more of the following conditions to have an active diagnosis:; Total calcium level greater than 12 mg/dL (3-3.5 mmol/L);; Ionized calcium level greater than 5.6 mg/dL (2-2.5 mmol/L);; Creatinine clearance less than 60 mL/min; or; Bone mineral density T-score less than 2.5 SD (below the mean).; Decreased renal function; Digestive conditions; Bone fractures; Heart issues; Kidney stones; Vision problems

Name: Underactive Parathyroid (Hypoparathyroidism)
Description: Parathyroid produces too few hormones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Cataracts; Congestive heart failure; Decreased renal function; Kidney stones; Return to top; Helps with cell reproduction and releases growth hormone.

Name: Acromegaly (Gigantism)
Description: Pituitary gland produces too much growth hormone.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; Produces a variety of hormones including adrenaline and the steroids aldosterone and cortisol.

Name: Cushing’s Syndrome (Hypercortisolism)
Description: When there is too much cortisol in the body.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; ALL Ratings under this schedule are TEMPORARY lasting for 6 months following INITIAL diagnosis; After which, the Veteran will be rated on any residuals.; Bone fractures; Diabetes mellitus; Erectile dysfunction; Headaches; High blood pressure; Mental disorders

Name: Diabetes Insipidus (DI)
Description: When the body can't regulate how it handles fluids. The condition is caused by a hormonal abnormality and isn't actually related to type 1 or 2 diabetes.

Name: Addison’s Disease (Adrenocortical Insufficiency, Hypocortisolism)
Description: Adrenal glands not producing enough steroid hormones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; If caused by a tuberculosis infection "Tuberculous Addison's Disease". The Tuberculous schedule will be used instead. Once that schedule has been exhausted, the Veteran will be rated on any residuals. Which, if the Addison's disease is still present the Veteran may then be rated under this schedule.; An Addison's “crisis” is when: arteries and veins in the limbs collapse and blood can no longer circulate throughout the limbs. This is causes extremely low blood pressure and severe shock. Other symptoms can include nausea, vomiting, anorexia, dehydration, severe weakness, pain in the stomach, legs, and back, fever, and reduced brain functioning which can lead to coma and even death.; An Addison's “episode” is when: arteries and veins do not collapse, but there are symptoms including anorexia, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, dehydration, weakness, low blood pressure, and low blood sugar.; Return to top

Name: Polyglandular Syndrome (Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia, Autoimmune Polyglandular Syndrome)
Description: When a condition affects multiple glands of the endocrine system at the same time.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Rated based upon the main symptoms the condition causes:; So, Diabetes mellitus, Hyperthyroidism, Hypothyroidism, Hypoparathyroidism, etc.; Return to top

Name: Cancer (Malignant Neoplasms)
Description: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Thyroid cancer: if exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Tumor (Benign Neoplasm)
Description: Has no rating in and of itself.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: However, if the Veteran suffers residual symptoms due to the presence of the tumor(s) or any treatments used to deal with them. Those may be rated.

Name: Prolactin Secreting Pituitary Dysfunction (Hyperpituitarism)
Description: When the pituitary gland produces too many hormones because of a tumor or cancer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: It is rated under the appropriate Tumor or cancer schedules - whichever is the reason for the overproduction.

Name: Hyperaldosteronism
Description: When the adrenal glands produce too many aldosterone hormones because of a tumor or cancer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: It is rated under the appropriate Tumor or cancer schedules - whichever is the reason for the overproduction.

Name: Pheochromocytoma (PCC)
Description: When the adrenal glands produce too many noradrenaline and adrenaline hormones because of a tumor or cancer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: It is rated under the appropriate Tumor or cancer schedules - whichever is the reason for the overproduction.

Name: C-cell Hyperplasia of the Thyroid
Description: When the C-cells that produce calcitonin in the thyroid grow abnormally into a cancer.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If it is treated with antineoplastic therapy/chemotherapy, it is rated as Cancer.; If surgery is performed and antineoplastic therapy/chemotherapy is NOT required, then it is rated as Hypothyroidism.; Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veteran's may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. Name: Collar Bone (Clavicle)
Description: When the collar bone dislocates from the shoulder bone (scapula).
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; When things go missing, break, or never heal.; PYRAMIDING NOTES:; If the bone(s) impact ROM, then separate evaluations for ROM and dislocation are NOT allowed; you will get the higher of the 2 evaluations.; If the bone does NOT impact ROM, then you CAN have a separate evaluation for ROM AND dislocation.; If BOTH bones healed poorly and/or were removed AND BOTH do NOT impact ROM, then separate evaluations CAN be awarded for BOTH bones AND ROM.; Separate grants for collar bone/shoulder bone dislocation and collar bone/shoulder bone healing issues are NOT allowed.

Name: Upper Arm (Humerus Bone)
Description: Funniest bone in the body.

Name: Collar Bone (Clavicle) or Shoulder Bone (Scapula)
Description: The following ratings and descriptions apply to both of these bones. The Veteran does not need to have both bones affected.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; The Veteran will NOT receive an additional rating if they fit the criteria of any of these miscellaneous cases. The Veteran will get a single rating for the ROM or Miscellaneous rating; Whichever rating is higher!; Pain with Motion; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; When there is an award based on pain with motion with movement of the shoulder. The VA will only award a SINGLE rating under range of motion! No matter how many ranges of motion are impacted!; Arthritis; When a Veteran's arthritis does not warrant a rating based upon ROM or other miscellaneous situations. The Veteran's condition will be rated under degenerative arthritis.; For an explanation of arthritis ratings click HERE.; Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veteran's may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. Name: Chronic Bronchitis
Description: Swelling of the bronchi in the lungs.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Bronchiectasis
Description: When part of the bronchial tree becomes enlarged, obstructing airflow.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Rated under the General Rating Schedule or on incapacitating episodes; Whichever gives the Veteran the highest rating.; IMPORTANT NOTE:; Incapacitating episode is defined as: requiring bed rest and treatment by a physician.

Name: Asthma
Description: Swollen airways and bronchi.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Note:; In the absence of clinical findings of asthma at time of examination, a verified history of asthmatic attacks must be of record.; The Veteran will receive a singular rating, based upon the below tables - whichever is highest.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Bronchodilators:; AccuNeb; Albuterol; Alupent; Bambuterol; Bitolterol mesylate; Bricanyl; Clenbuterol; Fenoterol; Foradil; Formoterol; Indacaterol; Levalbuteral; Levosalbutamol; Maxair; Metaproterenol; Pirbuteral; Procaterol; Proventil; Ritodrine; Salbutamol; Salmeterol; Sereveut; Symbicort; Terbutaline; Ventolin; Xopenex; Immunosuppressive Medications:; Decadron; Deltasone; Dexamethasone; Medrol; Orasone; Pediapred; Prednisone; Prednisolone; Prelone; Inhaled Anti-inflammatory Medications:; Advair (Fluticasone and Salmeterol); Aerobid (Flunisolide); Asmanex; Azmacort (triamcinolone); Dulera; Flovent HFA (Flutocasone HFA); Ipratropium Bromide (Atrovent); Pulmicort; Qvar; Symbicort

Name: Pulmonary Emphysema
Description: Disease that causes the tissues that maintain the shape of the lungs to die.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)
Description: Having chronic bronchitis and/or emphysema.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Rated under the General Rating Schedule.; Return to top; Diseases that limit how much the lungs can expand.

Name: Diaphragm Paralysis (Diaphragm Paresis)
Description: Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Injury of the Spinal Cord (Spinal Cord Injury with Respiratory Insufficiency)
Description: Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Kyphoscoliosis, Pectus Excavatum, Pectus Carinatum
Description: Kyphoscoliosis - abnormal curving of the spine both side-to-side & back-to-front.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Pectus excavatum - ribs and sternum grow inward, causing a caved-in chest.; Pectus carinatum - ribs and sternum grow outward in the middle of the chest.; These conditions limit the space in which the lungs can expand.; Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Collapsed Lung (Pneumothorax), Gunshot Wound to the Lungs (Traumatic Chest Wall Defect), Hernia, etc.
Description: Conditions or defects of the chest wall that keep the lungs from inflating properly.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Hernia - when the lung pushes through the chest wall.; Collapsed lung (Pneumothorax); PYRAMIDING NOTES:; If the muscles in Group XXI are affected (they control breathing). The Veteran will receive a respiratory rating OR a muscular rating - whichever is higher.; If other muscle groups are affected the Veteran can receive ratings for BOTH.

Name: Removal of a Lung (Lobectomy, Pneumonectomy, etc.)
Description: Rated under the General Rating Schedule.

Name: Chronic Pleural Effusion, Fibrosis, or Pleurisy with Empyema
Description: Effusion - excess fluid build up between the lung and the chest wall.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Fibrosis - excess connective tissues build up between the lung and the chest wall.; Pleurisy with empyema - pus builds up between the lung and the chest wall.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Sarcoidosis
Description: When lumps form on the tissues surrounding the air sacs, bronchi and small blood vessels in the lungs.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Return to top; Conditions that affect the tissues surrounding the air sacs in the lungs:

Name: Diffuse Interstitial Fibrosis (Interstitial Pneumonitis, Fibrosing Alveolitis)
Asbestosis
Description: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If exposed to Burn pits.; Conditions attributed to drugs, radiation, asbestos rebuts this presumptive.; They are all rated under the following schedule:; Exercise Test; DLCO (SB); FVC; Heart Test; Miscellaneous; Return to top; Bacterial infections of the lungs:

Name: Chronic Lung Abscess
Description: Rated under the following schedule:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; Caused by inhaling fungal spores:

Name: Coccidioidomycosis
Description: It can take several years before this condition manifests. As such, if you are filing a claim for this condition it is important that your records show service in the Southwest US, Central America, or South America.

Name: Mucormycosis
Description: They are all rated under the following schedule:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top

Name: Deviation of the Nasal Septum (Nasal Septum Deviation)
Description: When your nasal septum is significantly displaced to one side, making one nasal air passage smaller than the other.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTE:; You CANNOT get separate evaluations for deviated septum and allergic or bacterial rhinitis.; IMPORTANT NOTE:; This condition can only be rated IF it is a result of a traumatic injury!

Name: Loss of Part of the Nose/Significant Scarring
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If the Veteran would rate higher under Disfiguring scars they will be rated under that schedule instead of this one.

Name: Chronic Sinusitis (Various)
Description: When the sinuses swell, often caused by an infection or allergy.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTES:; You CAN get separate evaluations for rhinitis and sinusitis!; Sinus headaches CANNOT be awarded a separate evaluation and/or be rated under the Migraine schedule, even if the migraine schedule would give a higher evaluation! Typically a grant would look like ‘chronic sinusitis with sinus headaches’.; The exception to being rated under the migraine schedule would require the rater to exercise an Extra scheduler evaluation or a BVA or higher judge exercising judicial judgement.; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; NOTES:; Remember, the sinusitis must be chronic or it cannot be rated. So if you get a diagnosis of 'acute' that is NOT going to fly!; However, do know that a diagnosis of recurrent acute rhinosinusitis (RARS) IS a chronic condition, despite the name.; Incapacitating episode means the Veteran required bed rest and treatment by a physician.; All sinusitis conditions are rated the same. This includes:; Ethmoid sinusitis; Frontal sinusitis; Maxillary sinusitis; Pansinusitis; Sphenoid sinusitis; Common Secondary Condition:; Obstructive Sleep Apnea

Name: Allergic or Vasomotor Rhinitis
Description: Swelling of the tissues lining the nasal passage caused by allergies or other environmental triggers.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTE:; You CAN get separate evaluations for rhinitis and sinusitis!; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Common Secondary Condition:; Obstructive Sleep Apnea

Name: Bacterial Rhinitis
Description: Swelling of the tissues lining the nasal passage caused by a bacterial infection.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTE:; You CAN get separate evaluations for rhinitis and sinusitis!; PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Granulomatous Rhinitis
Description: Swelling of the tissues lining the nasal passage with abnormal cell growths not caused by bacteria.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:; If exposed to Burn pits.; Return to top; Made up of the pharynx, larynx and trachea.

Name: Chronic Laryngitis
Description: Swelling of the larynx (lasting at least 3 months at a time).

Name: Loss of Organic Speech (Aphonia, Inability to Speak)
Description: IMPORTANT NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: If the inability to speak is due to a Mental disorder and not a physical (to include nerve based) reason it CANNOT be rated under this schedule!; NOTE:; If the Veteran's inability to speak comes and goes then they will be rated under Chronic laryngitis.

Name: Stenosis of the Larynx
Description: Narrowing of the larynx.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; If the Veteran's condition results in the chronic inability to speak it can be rated under Aphonia instead - if it results in a higher rating.

Name: Injuries of the Pharynx
Description: Return to top

Name: Pulmonary Vascular Disease
Description: Affects the blood vessels leading to and from the lungs.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Upper Airway Resistance Syndrome (UARS); Represents a progression toward the potential development of sleep apnea, caused by snoring.; However, UARS, in and of itself, does NOT meet the criteria of sleep-disordered breathing that defines sleep apnea and is NOT considered a ratable disability for compensation purposes.; If you have a diagnosis for UARS, you should wait on submitting a Claim till/if you get a diagnosis of sleep apnea.

Name: Sleep Apnea (SA, Obstructive, Central, Mixed)
Description: When you stop breathing while you sleep or your breathing becomes insufficient leading to low blood oxygen levels.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; The VA MUST have a sleep study! Otherwise they CANNOT grant service-connection.; Home sleep studies are acceptable if:; It has been clinically determined that the Veteran can be appropriately evaluated by a home sleep study; and; A competent medical provider has evaluated the results.; The Veteran only needs to be prescribed a CPAP machine to rate 50%. While it would be a good idea to use it, for your health and all. It is NOT a requirement to use it to maintain this rating.; NOTES:; Other qualifying breathing assistance devices include:; Other positive airway pressure machines (automatic positive airway pressure device (APAP); bilevel positive airway pressure device (BiPAP));; Positive airway pressure machines may also be called non-invasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) or non-invasive ventilation (NIV).; Nasopharyngeal appliances (nasal dilators; nasopharyngeal stents);; Oral appliances (mandibular advancement devices (MAD)); tongue-retaining mouthpieces); and; Implanted genioglossal nerve stimulation devices.; Return to top

Name: Cancer (Malignant Tumor)
Description: PRESUMPTIVE CONDITION:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Cancers of: lung, bronchus, larynx, and trachea; if exposed to Agent Orange.; Breathing related cancers; if exposed to Burn pits.

Name: Benign Tumor
Description: Having a tumor itself is not a compensational condition.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: However, if the tumor causes Secondary conditions such as Aphonia. Those issues may be rated. As well as any resulting scars that are the result of the removal of any tumor(s).; Return to top; To get an idea of how a C&P exam will be conducted it is recommended that the Veteran look at the applicable DBQ.; Veteran's may ask a physician to complete a DBQ on their behalf to submit with their claim. Name: Soft Tissue Injury of the Mouth (Other than Tongue or Lips)
Description: Rated as Disfigurement of the face, head, or neck, Painful/unstable scar(s), and/or Impairment of eating.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; Conditions such as the Loss of teeth, Loss of the palate, and jaw are rated under the Dental schedule.

Name: Lips, Injuries of
Description: Rated as Disfigurement of the face, head, or neck and/or Painful/unstable scar(s).

Name: Loss of Tongue, Whole or Part (True Aglossia)
Description: Muscles in your neck, shoulders, and upper body spasm regularly.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; Prescription must be made by a medical provider.; Common Secondary Condition; Speech impairment rated as either:; Complete organic aphonia; or; Laryngitis, chronic.

Name: Loss of Sense of Taste (Ageusia)
Description: Return to top
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Connects the mouth to the stomach.

Name: Difficulty Swallowing (Stricture of the Esophagus, Dysphagia)
Description: When the esophagus narrows/tightens, making it hard to swallow.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; Esophagitis, mechanical or chemical;; Esophagitis due to peptic stricture;; Esophagitis due to radiation therapy;; Drug induced or infectious esophagitis due to Candida, virus, or other organism;; Idiopathic eosinophilic, or lymphocytic esophagitis;; Mallory Weiss syndrome (bleeding at junction of esophagus and stomach due to tears) due to caustic ingestion of alkali or acid; and; ANY esophageal condition that requires treatment with sclerotherapy.; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; CANNOT be rated together with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD).; IMPORTANT NOTES:; Condition MUST be documented by either:; Barium swallow;; Computerized tomography; or; Esophagogastroduodenoscopy.; Symptoms MUST be medically documented to be considered under this schedule.; NOTES:; Recurrent esophageal stricture is defined as the inability to maintain target esophageal diameter beyond 4 weeks after the target diameter has been achieved.; Refractory esophageal stricture is defined as the inability to achieve target esophageal diameter despite receiving no fewer than 5 dilatation sessions performed at 2-week intervals.

Name: Esophageal Motility Disorder (Achalasia, Spasms of the Esophagus, Cardiospasm)
Description: When muscles in the lower esophagus cannot move food down into the stomach.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; Achalasia (cardiospasm);; Corkscrew esophagus;; Diffuse esophageal spasm (DES);; Esophageal rings (including Schatzki rings);; Esophagus webs;; Mucosal webs or folds;; Nutcracker esophagus;; Other motor disorders of the esophagus; and; Impairment of the esophagus caused by systemic conditions such as myasthenia gravis, scleroderma, and other neurologic conditions.; Rated as Difficulty swallowing.

Name: Diverticulum of the Esophagus
Description: Bubble or pocket of stretched tissue that pushes through the muscle wall of the esophagus.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; Epiphrenic (distal esophagus);; Mid esophageal diverticulum; and; Pharyngo-esophageal diverticulum (Zenker's diverticulum).; Rated as Difficulty swallowing.

Name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
Description: When your stomach contents leak out and burn your food pipe.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: PYRAMIDING NOTE:; CANNOT be rated together with Difficulty swallowing.; IMPORTANT NOTES:; Condition MUST be documented by either:; Barium swallow;; Computerized tomography; or; Esophagogastroduodenoscopy.; Symptoms MUST be medically documented to be considered under this schedule.; NOTES:; Recurrent esophageal stricture is defined as the inability to maintain target esophageal diameter beyond 4 weeks after the target diameter has been achieved.; Refractory esophageal stricture is defined as the inability to achieve target esophageal diameter despite receiving no fewer than 5 dilatation sessions performed at 2-week intervals.

Name: Barrett's Esophagus
Description: Precancerous condition due to changes in the cellular structure of your food tube lining, usually due to repeated damage.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; If difficulty of swallowing is present then this condition is rated as Difficulty swallowing instead of this schedule.; MUST be diagnosed by pathologic testing; NOTE:; If this condition is fixed via surgery, radiofrequency ablation, or other treatment, the esophagus residuals are rated as Difficulty swallowing.; Return to top; Holds your food, as it breaks down.

Name: Chronic Gastritis
Description: Continual swelling of the stomach/glands.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies to:; Drug-induced gastritis;; Helicobacter pylori infection;; Portal-hypertensive gastropathy with varix-related complications; and; Zollinger-Ellison syndrome.; Rated as Peptic ulcer disease.

Name: Stenosis of the Stomach
Description: When the stomach becomes abnormally narrowed.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Rated as Chronic complications of upper gastrointestinal surgery or Peptic ulcer disease; whichever is the predominant disability.

Name: Injury of the Stomach
Description: Any residual symptoms are rated as Peritoneal adhesions.

Name: Removal of Stomach, Full/Partial (Gastrectomy)
Description: Rated as Chronic complications of upper gastrointestinal surgery.

Name: Removal of the Vegus Nerve, Partial or Full (Vagotomy)
Description: IMPORTANT NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: To get a rating under this schedule, the surgery MUST have been performed in addition with either a gastroenterostomy OR a pyloroplasty!; RATER NOTES:; Rate post-operative residuals not addressed by this diagnostic code under Chronic complications of upper gastrointestinal surgery.; If secondary service-connection is granted for Peptic ulcer disease due to the vagotomy; the MINIMUM evaluation for the ulcer is 20%!; Return to top; Pulls double duty as a gland that is involved in both the Endocrine system and the digestive system.; For the digestive system, the pancreas makes important enzymes that help the small intestines absorb nutrients.

Name: Pancreas Inflammation (Pancreatitis)
Description: IMPORTANT NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Abdominal pain MUST be confirmed by diagnostic studies to show the pain is due to the pancreatitis condition.; Diabetes mellitus due to pancreatic insufficency.

Name: Pancreas Transplant
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: You CANNOT get the minimum evaluation in addition to separate evaluation(s) for residual digestive symptom(s); you will get whichever is higher. Be it the minimum evaluation OR the residual digestive symptom(s).

Name: Removal of Part/All of the Pancreas (Pancreatecomy)
Description: Can alternatively be rated based on residuals:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Chronic complications of upper gastrointestinal surgery;; Irritable bowel syndrome;; Crohn's disease;; Removal of part/all the large intestine; or; Pancreatitis; whichever is the predominant disability.; PYRAMIDING NOTES:; You CANNOT get the minimum evaluation in addition to separate evaluation(s) for residual symptom(s); you will get whichever is higher. Be it the minimum evaluation OR the residual digestive symptom(s).; Return to top; Collects bile produced by the liver and passes it to the stomach when  digesting food. The bile specifically helps in the digesting of fats.

Name: Chronic Gallbladder Inflammation (Chronic Cholecystitis, Chronic Biliary Tract Disease)
Description: Usually due to gallstones (cholelithiasis).
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; Biliary structures;; Bile duct injury;; Cholangitis;; Choledochal cyst; and; Spinchter of Oddi dysfunction.; NOTE:; Primary sclerosing cholangitis is rated as Chronic Liver Disease (Without Cirrhosis).

Name: Chronic Gallstones (Chronic Cholelithiasis)
Description: When the gallbladder continually produces gallstones.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Rated as Chronic gallbladder inflammation.

Name: Injury to the Gallbladder
Description: Rated as Peritoneal adhesions, Chronic cholecystitis, or Removal of the gallbladder, depending on the predominant disability.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: RATER NOTE:; When rating gallbladder injuries analogous to Peritoneum adhesions, a finding of adhesions is NOT necessary.

Name: Removal of the Gallbladder (Cholecystectomy)
Description: Return to top
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Regulates metabolism, clears out old red blood cells, produces hormones and filters toxins out of the body. It also produces bile for the gallbladder.

Name: Cirrhosis of the Liver (Hepatic Cirrhosis, Primary Biliary Cirrhosis, and the Cirrhotic Phase of Sclerosing Cholangitis)
Description: Late-stage liver disease.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTE:; In order to get a rating under this schedule the diagnosis MUST be confirmed by biopsy, biochemical studies, or imaging; WITH abnormal liver function tests

Name: Chronic Liver Disease (Without Cirrhosis)
Description: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency;; Autoimmune hepatitis;; Chronic active hepatitis;; Drug-induced hepatitis;; Hemochromatosis;; Hepatitis B (HBV);; Non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH);; Primary biliary cirrhosis (PBC);; Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC); and; Wilson's disease; However, this does NOT include Hepatitis C; which has its own diagnostic code.; IMPORTANT NOTES:; If you are claiming Hepatitis B you MUST identify what risk factor(s) you had in service that could have resulted in you obtaining the condition!; If you have multiple risk factors that apply to you, then you should list them ALL!; If your ONLY risk factor is drug use in service then you CANNOT get service-connection!; Hepatitis B MUST be diagnosed via serologic testing.; Here are some examples of risk factors:; RATER NOTES:; For Veterans whose physicians recommend BOTH parenteral antiviral therapy AND parenteral immunomodulatory drugs, but such treatments are NOT safe for the Veteran, rate under the Cirrhosis of the liver schedule criteria.; If the Veteran was service-connected for hepatitis B AFTER October 31, 1990 and the grant was based on drug use - do NOT grant any increase claims for this condition! Further, if the condition is not protected by the 10 year rule propose severing service-connection!; SPECIAL RATER NOTES:; In the case of hepatitis C conditions rated under this schedule:; Do NOT assign a 0 percent evaluation if there are ANY symptoms, even mild - assign a 20% rating!; Do NOT assign a 0 percent evaluation if there is evidence of liver damage shown on any liver function tests - assign a minimum 20% rating!

Name: Hepatitis C (Hepatitis that is not A or B)
Description: Infection in the liver.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; If you are claiming Hepatitis C (HCV) you MUST identify what risk factor(s) you had in service that could have resulted in you obtaining the condition!; If you have multiple risk factors that apply to you, then you should list them ALL!; If your ONLY risk factor is drug use in service then you CANNOT get service connection!; Hepatitis C; MUST be diagnosed via serologic testing.; Here are some examples of risk factors:; Rated under Chronic Liver Disease (Without Cirrhosis).; RATER NOTES:; If the Veteran was service-connected AFTER October 31, 1990 and the grant was based on drug use - do NOT grant any increase claims for this condition! Further, if the condition is not protected by the 10 year rule propose severing service-connection!

Name: Injuries to the Liver
Description: Common causes of liver injury include:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Alcohol;; Drugs (Acetaminophen, isoniazid, phenytoin);; Physical trauma;; Toxic substances; and; Viral infections.; Rated under Peritoneal adhesions, Cirrhosis of the liver, and/or as Chronic liver disease without cirrhosis.

Name: Liver Abscess
Description: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to abscesses caused by:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Amebic (e.g., E. hystolytica);; Bacterial;; Fungal (e.g., C. albicans); and; Viral.

Name: Liver Transplant
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: You CANNOT get the minimum evaluation in addition to separate evaluation(s) for residual digestive symptom(s); you will get whichever is higher. Be it the minimum evaluation OR the residual digestive symptom(s).; Fatty Liver Disease (Hepatic Steatosis); This condition is NOT a disability for VA purposes - it is considered a lab finding.; As such, the VA CANNOT grant service-connection for it!; Return to top; There are two - large intestine (colon) and small intestine. The small intestine comes off the stomach where it then connects to the large intestines. The large intestines then empty out through your  rectum.

Name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS, Irritable Colon Syndrome, Spastic Colitis, Mucous Colitis, etc.)
Description: An intestinal disorder that causes pain in the belly, gas, diarrhea, and constipation.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Symptoms include:; Bloating; Irregular bowel movements (constipation or diarrhea); Stomach pain; SPECIAL NOTE:; Gulf War medically unexplained chronic multisymptom illness associated undiagnosed gastrointestinal issues are evaluated under this schedule or Gastrointestinal dysmotility syndrome; whichever provides the higher evaluation.

Name: Ulcerative Colitis (UC, Colitis Ulcerosa, Inflammatory Bowel Disease, IBD)
Description: Causes inflammation and ulcers (sores) in your digestive tract.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Rated as Crohn's disease.

Name: Chronic Enteritis
Description: Constant swelling of the small intestine.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Symptoms include:; Cramping; Dehydration; Diarrhea; Fever; Stomach pain; Rated as Irritable bowel syndrome or Crohn's disease or undifferentiated form of inflammatory bowel disease; whichever is the predominant disability.

Name: Crohn's Disease or Undifferentiated Form of Inflammatory Bowel Disease
Description: Constant swelling and irritation of the digestive track. May involve small bowel (ileitis), large bowel (colitis), or inflammation of any component of the gastrointestinal tract from the mouth to the anus.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; Condition MUST be diagnosed by endoscopy or radiologic studies.; Following colectomy/colostomy with persistent or recurrent symptoms, condition will be rated under this schedule or Removal of part/all the large intestine; whichever provides the highest rating.

Name: Diverticular Disease (Diverticulitis, Diverticulosis)
Description: A condition in which small, bulging pouches develop in the digestive tract.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Symptoms include:; Diarrhea; Fever; High white blood cell count; Stomach pain; IMPORTANT NOTE:; Following colectomy/colostomy with persistent or recurrent symptoms, condition will be rated under this schedule or Removal of part/all the large intestine; whichever provides the highest rating.

Name: Celiac Disease
Description: Intolerance of gluten.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: IMPORTANT NOTES:; MUST be diagnosed by serum antibody test or endoscopy WITH biopsy.; Condition will be rated under this schedule or Removal of part/all the small intestine; whichever provides the highest rating.

Name: Gastrointestinal Dysmotility Syndrome
Description: When the muscles of your intestines have difficulties working correctly.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: SPECIAL NOTE:; Gulf War medically unexplained chronic multisymptom illness associated undiagnosed gastrointestinal issues are evaluated under this schedule or Irritable bowel syndrome; whichever provides the higher evaluation.

Name: External Fistula of the Large or Small Intestine (Gastrointestinal Fistula, GIF, Intestinal fistulous Disease)
Description: An abnormal passageway from the intestine to the outside of the body.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; External fistulas that have developed as a consequence of abdominal trauma, surgery, radiation, malignancy, infection, or ischemia.

Name: Removal of Part/All the Small Intestine (Resection of the Small Intestine)
Description: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Mesenteric ischemic thrombosis;; Post-bariatric surgery complications;; Short bowel syndrome.; RATER NOTE:; If short bowel syndrome results in high output syndrome, to include high-output stoma, consider assigning a higher evaluation under Removal of part/all the large intestine.

Name: Removal of Part/All the Large Intestine (Resection of the Large Intestine, Colectomy)
Description: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Ostomy.; Return to top; Rectum, the end of the large intestine.; Anus, the end of the road.

Name: Incontinence, Complete or Partial loss (Poor Sphincter Control, Impairment of Sphincter Control)
Description: When the gates cannot quite hold back the...
Medical Terms and Symptoms: The inability to hold or release poop at an appropriate time AND place.; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; CAN be rated separately from other digestive issues. BARRING other rectum/anus conditions with overlapping symptoms.; SPECIAL NOTE:; If there is ANY loss of sphincter control AND the Veteran has Both legs amputated or completely paralyed the rating will be 100%.

Name: Anal Stenosis (Stricture of the Rectum or Anus)
Description: When scar tissue narrows the openings of the rectum or anus.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: This diagnostic code applies, but is NOT limited to:; Anismus (functional constipation); and; Dyssynergic defecation (levator ani).; PYRAMIDING NOTE:; CAN be rated separately from other digestive issues. BARRING other rectum/anus conditions with overlapping symptoms.

Name: Prolapse of the Rectum
Description: When the rectum slips out of place.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: SPECIAL NOTE:; If impairment of bowel control is the predominant disability, then condition will be rated under diagnostic code Incontinence instead.

Name: Anal Fistula (Anorectal Abscess, Anorectal Fistula, Fistula-in-Ano)
Description: Abnormal passageway connecting the anus to other internal parts of the body or outside the body

Name: Hemorrhoids (Piles)
Description: Swollen veins inside or outside, near the anus.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: RATER NOTE:; AFTER service-connection has been granted, assume that ANY future re-occurrence is related to service. UNLESS, there is clear and unmistakable evidence that the Veteran was originally granted service-connection in error.

Name: Itchy Anus (Anal Itching, Pruritus Ani)
Description: Persistent and often intense itch around the anus.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Return to top; Abdomen, the large cavity in which all the organs sit.; Peritoneum, the tissue that lines the abdomen just under the skin.

Name: Peritoneum Adhesions
Description: When scar tissue causes the surface of the peritoneum to stick to the  surface of other organs. This can cause those organs to twist/bend out out shape and reduce their ability to function.

Name: Chronic Complications of Upper Gastrointestinal Surgery
Description: This diagnostic code includes operations performed on the esophagus, stomach, pancreas, and small intestine, including bariatric surgery.
Medical Terms and Symptoms: NOTE:; If the surgery was the REMOVAL of a part of the small you will be rated under Removal of part/all the small intestine NOT this schedule.; Residual scar(s).; Pancreatic surgery often result in a vitamin or mineral deficiency (e.g., B12, iron, calcium, or fat-soluble vitamins). Which can lead to:; Avitaminosis Vitamin A, B, C or D deficiencies;; Keratitis or keratomalacia due to Vitamin A deficiency;; Neuropathy due to Vitamin E deficiency; and; Prolonged clotting due to Vitamin K deficiency.

Name: Tuberculosis of the Peritoneum (Peritoneum Tuberculosis, PTB)
Visceroptosis
Description: Prolapse or a sinking of the abdominal viscera (internal organs) below their natural positions.

Name: Hernia, including Femoral, Incisional, Inguinal, Umbilical, Ventral, and other (but not including Hiatal)
Description: PYRAMIDING NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: You CANNOT get multiple separate evaluations for hernias under this schedule.; IMPORTANT NOTES:; Due to there being 2 inguinal canals, it is possible for them both to herniate. In the event both are herniated and they BOTH would get a rating of at least 10% then the rating gets the following special treatment:; The higher of the 2 ratings gets an additional 10% added to it and the Veteran gets 1 individual evaluation. For example: if the ratings were 30% and 30% the Veteran would get a singular 40% rating (from the 10% bonus).; This special treatment CANNOT be applied if a single 100% evaluation is warranted for one of the hernias.; RATER NOTE:; If Service Treatment Records (STRs) note inguinal hernia, service-connection is warranted without a medical opinion. UNLESS, there is medical evidence dated before service or other sufficient medical evidence to determine that an Aggravation medical opinion is required. The return of the hernia during service is NOT alone enough evidence to indicate aggravation has occurred. You NEED that opinion!; NOTE:; Any one of the following activities of daily living are sufficient for evaluation: bathing, dressing, hygiene, and/or transfers.

Name: Bulimia nervosa
Description: NOTE:
Medical Terms and Symptoms: Incapacitating episodes are defined as bed rest and treatment prescribed by a physician.; Eating Disorder Weight Table; The following table applies to both genders.; Return to top; Intellectual and Personality Disorders are generally considered to be something a person is born with, not something that develops. (Having a large genetic or hereditary component, with negligible environmental factors.); Because of this, the VA does NOT consider these disorders as diseases or injuries for compensation purposes.; UNLESS, they are the result of another service-connected disability such as a Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI); OR your service made your disorder measurably worse than when you joined service (Aggravated).; IMPORTANT NOTE:; If service-connected, the VA will NOT issue secondary service-connection from any disabilities that are a result of these disorders, the exception being if the Veteran has another mental disorder that is service-connected and the VA can associate those disabilities to it.; Intellectual and personality disorders include but are not limited to:; Antisocial personality disorder; Attention deficit disorder; Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD); Autism spectrum disorder; Avoidant personality disorder; Borderline personality disorder; Child-onset fluency disorder (stuttering); Intellectual developmental disorder; Intellectual disability; Learning disabilities; Mental retardation; Narcissistic personality disorder; Schizoid personality disorder; Specified learning disorder; Tic disorder; Return to top; The following types and named disorders are explicitly recognized by the VA as being rateable. If your mental disorder is not listed, it will likely be rated Analogously. This includes sleeping disorders such as insomnia.; Speaking of insomnia...; IMPORTANT NOTES:; Insomnia is generally considered a symptom of another disability due to coexisting physical or mental conditions.; For example: major depressive disorder, pain, or another sleep disorder like Sleep apnea.; Service-connection CAN be established for insomnia in the absence of a known or established underlying cause IF there is:; An event in service (such as a diagnosis of primary insomnia in service);; A current diagnosis of primary insomnia; and; A Nexus establishing primary insomnia is connected to the event in service, and the condition is NOT associated with any other disease or injury.; EXCEPTION to the RULES; Only the Board of Veterans Appeals (BVA) and higher courts have the ability to grant secondary service-connection for insomnia!; Adjustment Disorders; Emotional or behavioral reaction to a stressful event or change in a person's life.
